errors.title: Amount currency mismatch
errors.explanation: This error occurs when trying to use multiple amounts together in an operation while they are expressed in different currencies.
errors.businessRule: All monetary operations must involve amounts expressed in the same currency.

diagnostics.possibleCause: Amounts were used in a monetary operation without having been converted to the same currency.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Verify whether all amounts involved in the operation were converted to a common currency before being used together.

diagnostics.possibleCause: Amounts expected to be expressed in the same currency were provided with different currencies.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Check the currencies associated with each amount and confirm whether a common currency was expected for this operation.

examples.detailedMessage: Failed to perform the monetary operation because the involved amounts are expressed in different currencies: 127.33 EUR and 57689 USD.

errors.title: Transaction date outside statement period
errors.explanation: This error occurs when trying to validate a bank statement file that contains one or more transactions dated outside the statement period.
errors.businessRule: All transactions must occur within the statement period.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The transaction date provided in the statement file is incorrect or inconsistent with the actual transaction date.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Verify the transaction date present in the input file and confirm its consistency with the actual transaction timeline.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The statement period defined in the file does not match the actual coverage period of the transactions.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Check whether the statement start and end dates in the file align with the period covered by the transactions.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The transaction was posted after the statement was generated but was mistakenly included in the file.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Determine whether late-posted transactions were included in the statement generation process.

diagnostics.possibleCause: An internal processing error caused the transaction date to be shifted during data transformation or import.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Examine the data import and transformation stages to confirm that transaction dates are preserved without alteration.

examples.detailedMessage: Transaction dated 2024-02-02 is outside the statement period [2024-01-05;2024-01-31].
examples.shortMessage: Transaction date is outside the statement period.

context.description: The date of the transaction being processed.

errors.title: Statement total amount mismatch
errors.explanation: This error occurs when trying to validate a bank statement file whose declared total amount does not match the sum of the individual transaction amounts.
errors.businessRule: The statement total amount must equal the sum of all transaction amounts included in the statement.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The total amount declared in the statement file does not match the sum of the individual transaction amounts.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Verify the declared total amount in the file and compare it with the sum of all transaction amounts.

diagnostics.possibleCause: One or more transactions are missing or duplicated in the statement file.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Check whether all expected transactions are present exactly once in the statement file.

diagnostics.possibleCause: A rounding or precision error occurred when calculating the statement total amount.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Examine how rounding and precision rules were applied when computing the statement total.

diagnostics.possibleCause: An internal processing error altered transaction amounts during file parsing or transformation.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Inspect the file parsing and transformation stages to confirm that transaction amounts remain unchanged.

examples.detailedMessage: The declared statement total amount (1250 EUR) does not match the computed total amount from transactions (1249.5 EUR).

errors.title: Temperature below absolute zero
errors.explanation: This error occurs when trying to instantiate a temperature with a value that is below absolute zero.
errors.businessRule: Temperature cannot go below absolute zero because absolute zero is the point where particles have minimum possible energy.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The value entered manually by a user is invalid.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Verify the value entered by the user and assess its compliance with domain rules.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The value received from an external system (API, message, etc.) is invalid.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Check the data provided by the upstream system and evaluate its validity against domain rules.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The value was loaded from corrupted or outdated persisted data.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Examine the persisted data source to determine whether stored values comply with current domain rules.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The value was computed internally without using domain-safe methods.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Inspect the internal computation logic to confirm that domain invariants are preserved.

diagnostics.possibleCause: The value originates from system configuration or defaults that are incorrect or outdated.
diagnostics.analysisHint: Review the relevant configuration or default parameters to assess their compliance with domain rules.

examples.detailedMessage: Failed to instantiate temperature: the value -1 K is below absolute zero.
Failed to instantiate temperature: the value -280 °C is below absolute zero.
